description: Full fine-tuning, continued pre-training, or any gradient-based update to the core LLM parameters.
reason_forbidden: The core LLM must remain frozen to ensure benchmark isolation and reproducibility. Any weight update invalidates Stage A results.

description: Applying low-rank adaptation (LoRA) adapters to the core LLM without going through the evaluation workflow and deployment gates.
reason_forbidden: LoRA adapters modify core model behavior via injected weights; even small adapters can shift predictions in untestable ways and bypass the frozen-model invariant.

description: Allowing the system to modify its own core logic, prompt templates, or inference pipeline based on live feedback without human review or evaluation gate passage.
reason_forbidden: Self-modification creates untracked state changes that cannot be rolled back or audited. It violates the evaluation-required-before-promotion invariant.

description: Promoting any learned policy (ranking, scoring, classification, calibration) to active status without first passing the evaluation workflow and deployment gates.
reason_forbidden: Unverified promotion can silently degrade system quality. All policy changes must be validated against the deployment gate contract before activation.

description: Any change to the model's internal state — including cache poisoning, embedding drift, silent adapter loading, or prompt injection — that is not tracked in the evaluation log.
reason_forbidden: Hidden state changes make benchmark results non-reproducible and violate the transparency requirement of the BenchCore benchmark pack.
